mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction, reducing aggregate consumption and corporate profit growth, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure, forcing monetary tightening expectations that compress equity multiples and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint acceleration in retail sales and CPI yields positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a positive month-over-month change in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production changes coincide with negative 5-day returns in > 65% of recovery phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and driving positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate with 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year contraction in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction sector activity, transmitting negative sentiment to broader equity markets over the following month.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline by > 10% YoY but equity returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where total nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate falls indicates robust labor market absorption and rising wage income, boosting consumer spending and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth combined with falling unemployment yields negative 5-day returns in > 55% of cases during mid-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds the 3% threshold during a monetary tightening cycle, central bank hawkishness overrides growth optimism, triggering risk-off flows and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Inflation above 3% coincides with positive 20-day returns in > 60% of tightening cycle observations.
